hops.context: Token-Efficient Attack (Ours) Simple Standalone Arithmetic Tasks

Our research investigated whether simpler problem types could achieve the same vulnerability triggering effect. Contrary to previous assumptions, we discovered that simple standalone arithmetic tasks, such as the subtraction problem shown in the right portion of Figure 2, can successfully trigger the thinking-stopped vulnerability while maintaining high attack success rates.

question: In the context of the Token Compression Optimization, how does it contribute to the performance of Token-Efficient Attack on different task complexities?

options.A: It enhances the ability to maintain high attack success rates on simple standalone arithmetic tasks, outperforming complex mathematical word problems.
options.B: It only marginally improves attack success rates on complex mathematical word problems without affecting simple arithmetic tasks.
options.C: It causes both simple standalone arithmetic tasks and complex mathematical word problems to have decreased vulnerability triggering effects.
options.D: It significantly reduces the effectiveness of attacks on complex mathematical word problems, while improving performance on simple arithmetic tasks.

explanation: The Token Compression Optimization, as part of the Token-Efficient Attack, is particularly effective when applied to simple standalone arithmetic tasks, which unexpectedly outperform complex mathematical word problems in terms of triggering vulnerabilities and maintaining high success rates. Option A is correct as it encapsulates the effectiveness of Token Compression Optimization in enhancing attack performance on simpler tasks compared to more complex ones.
